𝗪𝗜𝗙𝗜𝗙𝗢𝗥𝗚𝗘 | Training cybersecurity professionals and students on Wi-Fi attack vectors and wireless capabilities in a simulated environment.  🌟 Rising Fast!

◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🐍 Python 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Training cybersecurity professionals and students on Wi-Fi attack vectors and wireless capabilities in a simulated environment.
📅 Created: May 2024 • Updated: Jul 2026

◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Safe and legal Wi-Fi hacking training environment
✅ Automated network and tool setup for labs
✅ Based on open-source mininet-wifi
✅ Removes need for physical hardware for attacks
✅ Supports various Wi-Fi exploitation labs


◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐⭐ 1K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 130 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 3 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

WifiForge is a Python-based tool designed to provide a safe and legal environment for learning Wi-Fi hacking techniques. It automates the setup of networks and tools required for various Wi-Fi exploitation labs, leveraging the open-source mininet-wifi framework.

◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉◉

𝗗𝗢𝗖𝗞𝗘𝗥𝗦𝗖𝗔𝗡 | Scanning Docker containers and images for security vulnerabilities, compliance issues, and hidden threats.  📈 Growing!

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ CIS Docker Benchmark v1.7.0 compliance checking
✅ Supply Chain Attack Detection (Imageless Container, Cryptocurrency Miner, Backdoored Library, Image Signature Verification, Phishing Content, Malicious Network Destinations)
✅ Advanced Secrets Detection (Cloud Providers, Version Control, AI/ML APIs)
✅ Written in Go for speed and concurrency
✅ Extensible plugin architecture
✅ SARIF output for CI/CD integration
✅ Exit codes for automation

DockerScan v2.0 is a comprehensive security scanner for Docker containers and images, rewritten in Go. It integrates multiple scanning techniques, including CIS benchmarks, supply chain attack detection, and advanced secrets detection, to ensure container security and compliance.

❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀❀

𝗧𝗨𝗫𝗠𝗔𝗧𝗘 | Simplifying and standardizing the process of installing applications across different Linux distributions by generating safe, inspectable installation commands.  🔥 Super Hot!

⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 📘 TypeScript 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Simplifying and standardizing the process of installing applications across different Linux distributions by generating safe, inspectable installation commands.
📅 Created: Dec 2025 • Updated: Jul 2026

⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Cross-distro generation (apt, pacman, dnf, zypper, nix, Flatpak, Snap, Homebrew)
✅ Native-first resolution with fallbacks
✅ Safe generation with inspectable output and package checks
✅ Parallel Flatpak installs
✅ Homebrew-aware output
✅ Clean terminal UX with colored logs and progress
✅ Verification features (AUR detection, Nix unfree checks, verified badges)
✅ Productive UI with copy/download drawer and distro-aware context
✅ Keyboard-first navigation
✅ PWA support


⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

TuxMate is a cross-distro command generator for installing applications on Linux. It allows users to select applications once and generate inspectable installation commands or scripts tailored for various Linux distributions and package managers, ensuring safe and understandable execution.

⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯

𝗗𝗜𝗥𝟮𝗧𝗫𝗧 | Consolidating entire project codebases into a single text file for analysis by Large Language Models (LLMs), code reviews, or archival purposes.  📈 Growing!

•••••••••••••••••••••••

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🐍 Python 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Consolidating entire project codebases into a single text file for analysis by Large Language Models (LLMs), code reviews, or archival purposes.
📅 Created: Jun 2026 • Updated: Jul 2026

•••••••••••••••••••••••

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Recursively scans all files in a directory
✅ Skips binary files
✅ Supports .dir2txtignore for custom ignore patterns
✅ Automatically removes code comments
✅ Estimates token count and checks against model limits
✅ Outputs consolidated project content to a single text file


•••••••••••••••••••••••

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐ 12 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 2 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 2 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

•••••••••••••••••••••••

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

dir2txt is a Python script that recursively scans a project directory, extracts the content of all non-binary files, and consolidates it into a single text file. It offers features like ignoring specific files, removing code comments, and estimating token counts, making it useful for feeding codebases to LLMs, code reviews, or creating project backups.

•••••••••••••••••••••••

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/unikOne56/dir2txt

𝗖𝗢𝗡𝗦𝗧𝗥𝗜𝗖𝗧 | Compressing video files to meet specific size limits for uploads or storage, without relying on online services.  🔥 Super Hot!

▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🐍 Python 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Compressing video files to meet specific size limits for uploads or storage, without relying on online services.
📅 Created: Dec 2024 • Updated: Jul 2026

▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Compress videos to target file sizes
✅ Automatic calculation of ABR, resolution, framerate, and audio quality
✅ Bulk compression
✅ Customizable framerate limits
✅ Choice of codecs (H.264, HEVC, AV1, VP9)
✅ Intuitive, easy-to-use interface
✅ Local processing


▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐ 641 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 41 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 0 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Constrict is a desktop application designed to compress video files to a specific target file size. It automates the process of determining optimal encoding parameters like bitrate, resolution, and framerate, offering a user-friendly interface for bulk video compression locally.

▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪▪
